architecture.summary: Reasoning-to-Defend (R2D) integrates safety-aware reasoning into LLM generation by introducing PIVOT TOKENS that signal safety status at each reasoning step. During generation, the model self-evaluates and dynamically adjusts response strategies based on these safety indicators. Contrastive Pivot Optimization (CPO) trains the model to better predict PIVOT TOKENS by contrasting safe and unsafe dialogue states, enabling the LRM to defend against jailbreak attacks while maintaining task performance.

biblio.title: Reasoning-to-Defend: Safety-Aware Reasoning Can Defend Large Language Models from Jailbreaking

claims.statement: R2D effectively mitigates various attacks and improves overall safety, while maintaining the original performances

Safety-aware reasoning at each step of the reasoning process enables LLMs to dynamically adjust their response strategies during reasoning, significantly enhancing their safety capabilities defending jailbreak attacks
Contrastive Pivot Optimization enhances the model's perception of the safety status of given dialogues to improve the accuracy of predicting PIVOT TOKENS
Large Reasoning Models benefit from enhanced reasoning capabilities against jailbreak queries
